gaps: The committed statpack lacks the 'Modern discretionary-cert petitions by disposition' section the predict prompt tells cert predictors to anchor on; only overall/by-court/by-topic/by-originating-circuit/by-Term sections are present.
The configured CourtListener MCP server never surfaced tools in this cell, so no MCP retrieval was possible.

A conditional base rate for petitions with a call for response (CFR) would be the single most useful cut for cert prediction — the CFR is the dominant docket signal here and I had to weigh it from general knowledge.
fedcourts query compact rows carried null caption/term_year for these SCOTUS priors, making relevance hard to judge without --full.
fedcourts query --citation matches cited authorities, not statutes; a statute filter would help find same-offense priors.
helpful: Provisioned petition.txt and questions-presented.txt (the whole merits analysis rests on them)
Snapshot proceedings text (the waiver -> CFR -> extension sequence)
fedcourts query ranged priors (7 denied / 1 granted sanity check)
notes: Forward-mode cert cell. Prediction rests on the provisioned petition + docket CFR signal; retrieval was degraded (no MCP) but not blocking.
